title: Artifact Transformer. Asm(java 8) cannot parse java module info(java 9+) classes
author: sumeru2
body:
Hello! I need assistance in resolving an issue related to artifact transformation in Gradle. I've encountered an error due to ASM (Java 8) being unable to process classes with Java module information (Java 9+).  If you have the opportunity to analyze the code and identify potential issues, I would appreciate your help. Thank you!   snippets of code: build.gradle - https://hastebin.com/share/nujemijoyu.javascript buildSrc/src/main/groovy/org/example/JavaModuleTransform.groovy - https://hastebin.com/share/ofiwevahow.java
Errors:
https://pastebin.com/pbnBXt0C
Java 8 Gradle 8.1.1

title: pluginManagement block must appear before any other statement
author: blundell
body:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/655860/135731097-c34c3fe6-e3d7-400c-b790-b2bf44a7b437.png)

^ when I try the above in my Android project I get:

```
> startup failed:
  settings file '/path/to/my-app/settings.gradle': 3: 
The pluginManagement {} block must appear before any other statements in the script.
```

and so I have to move the `rootProject.name` line below the `pluginManagement` block

But with such a simple setup, why is mine acting different to yours?

title: Same structure for an Android Project?
author: blundell
body:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/655860/135729900-01db4fbc-f265-4534-953f-2d7e2fd1cb1b.png)

I can't get this structure to work for an Android project. Do you expect it to?

as in, having 3 projects in AndroidStudio (`my-project`(android), `my-other-project`, `my-build-logic`) at the root. I get errors about it "not being a gradle based project".

The way I have seen it before is having the android app as root and other folders(projects) inside like:

/my-project (android)
--/my-build-logic
